name: Vigo
description_short: Western Union agent location network offering money transfers, cash pickup, bill payment, and check cashing through partner merchants across the United States.
description_long:
Western Union is a global money transfer service that operates through an extensive agent network in the United States. The company specializes in enabling consumers to send money domestically and internationally, with the ability to have funds picked up at thousands of agent locations. The service has been a long-standing solution for remittances, international transfers, and domestic cash movement.

Vigo's website displays Western Union's core service offerings, which include sending money, picking up cash transfers, tracking transfers in real-time, paying bills, accessing prepaid card services, and mobile top-up capabilities. The platform also offers inmate payment services and the ability to receive money directly onto prepaid cards. Western Union Rewards provides incentive-based pricing for frequent users, and the service supports multiple languages including English, Spanish, Haitian Creole, Chinese, French, and Vietnamese to serve diverse immigrant communities.

What distinguishes Western Union is its massive agent network—the sample location list shows agents operating through local businesses like Mexican markets, gift shops, check-cashing stores, and tax preparation offices. This hybrid model allows Western Union to maintain physical presence in underserved communities without operating traditional branches. The company's multi-language support and inmate payment services indicate focus on serving immigrant populations and those with limited banking access.

However, the website content reveals limited transparency about pricing, fees, and exchange rates—critical information for consumers evaluating money transfer costs. The agent-based model means service quality and hours vary significantly by location, as evidenced by the different opening times listed (8 AM to 11 AM). No information is provided about speed of transfers, compliance protections, or dispute resolution processes on the public-facing website.
address: Vigo, 2025 W 8th St, Los Angeles, CA 90057

company_info.headquarters: Vigo, 2025 W 8th St, Los Angeles, CA 90057

pros: Extensive agent network with 46+ locations visible in sample search, enabling convenient local cash pickup
Multi-language support in 6 languages (English, Spanish, Haitian Creole, Chinese, French, Vietnamese) for immigrant communities
Multiple service options including money transfers, bill payment, mobile top-up, and inmate payments
Real-time transfer tracking capability for monitoring fund movement
Western Union Rewards program offering incentive-based pricing for frequent users
Ability to receive money directly onto prepaid cards for unbanked consumers
Mobile and online access alongside in-person agent locations
cons: No pricing or fee information visible on website—consumers cannot compare costs before using service
Agent network quality and service hours vary significantly by location, ranging from 8 AM to 11 AM openings
Limited transparency about transfer speed, delivery timeframes, or processing times
No information provided about dispute resolution, fraud protection, or consumer safeguards
Heavy reliance on third-party agents reduces direct quality control and customer service consistency
best_for: Immigrants and expatriates sending money to family members in home countries
Unbanked and underbanked consumers without access to traditional bank accounts
Users in underserved communities where Western Union agent locations are established
Individuals needing immediate cash access through multiple convenient pickup locations
diagnosis: Western Union through Vigo is best for immigrants, unbanked consumers, and anyone needing convenient in-person cash pickup across a wide agent network. The primary caveat is complete lack of pricing transparency—consumers must contact individual agents to learn transfer costs and fees before committing to the service, making it difficult to compare against competitors.
services: Domestic money transfers within the United States
International money transfer and remittance services
Mobile top-up and phone credit purchases
